findings.title: Empty RETENTION_TICK_SECRET bypasses auth check
findings.why_it_matters: Deno.env.get() returns an empty string '' for an env var that is set to empty. In JavaScript, '' is falsy, so the if (RETENTION_TICK_SECRET) block is skipped. Any caller can trigger the retention job (which deletes chat_conversations rows and purges storage) without authentication. This is only exploitable if someone misconfigures the env var as empty rather than not-set, but the consequence is an unauthenticated data-deletion endpoint.
findings.suggested_fix: Change the guard: if (RETENTION_TICK_SECRET && RETENTION_TICK_SECRET.length > 0) or use RETENTION_TICK_SECRET?.trim(). The warning path should also fire for empty-string case.
evidence: Line 365: if (RETENTION_TICK_SECRET) { — empty string is falsy, skips auth block
Deno.env.get() returns '' for empty env var: https://deno.land/api?s=Deno.env

residual_risks: GET method is accepted alongside POST. Cloudflare cron uses POST per runbook, but GET acceptance means any URL fetcher without a body (e.g., monitoring probes) could trigger the job if RETENTION_TICK_SECRET is not set. Low risk given the secret guard.
testing_gaps: No test verifies that an empty-string RETENTION_TICK_SECRET leaves the endpoint unprotected
